summary: Router misroutes requests when replica set is empty
description: During a rolling restart where every replica briefly reported unhealthy, pick() in src/serving/router.ts threw an unhandled error instead of the API layer returning a clean 503-equivalent response to the caller.

comments.author: Owen Reyes
comments.body: pick() correctly throws when the healthy pool is empty, that part is by design. The gap is in src/api/http.ts, which does not catch that specific error and instead lets it bubble as an unhandled rejection.

comments.author: Owen Reyes
comments.body: Added a catch in handleRequest that maps a no-healthy-replicas error to a clean service-unavailable response instead of crashing the request handler. Tested with a forced empty replica set. Resolving.

comments.author: Elena Petrova
comments.body: Confirmed the rolling restart no longer produces unhandled errors in the logs. Thanks.
